label: Regex
description: Apply a regex pattern to a string. Supports match, replace, and test modes. Replaces the JS-expression-only path through `transform` for pattern extraction and substitution.

settings.name: input
settings.label: Input (template or upstream)
settings.description: Path-template to the source string. Omit to use the upstream connection's input directly.

settings.name: pattern
settings.label: Pattern
settings.description: Regex source string without the surrounding //. Use $1, $2 etc. in replacement to refer to capture groups.

settings.name: flags
settings.label: Flags
settings.description: Optional regex flags. 'g' = match all occurrences (required for replace to substitute every match), 'i' = case-insensitive, 'm' = multiline.

settings.name: mode
settings.label: Mode
settings.description: match = collect all matches and their capture groups; replace = substitute matches with replacement; test = boolean does-it-match check.

settings.name: replacement
settings.label: Replacement (replace mode only)
settings.description: Replacement string for mode='replace'. Reference capture groups with $1, $2, ...

ai.shortDescription: Match, replace, or test with a regex pattern.
ai.whenToUse: When you need to extract structured data from unstructured text (emails, IDs, URLs) or scrub PII before passing to the next node.
ai.examplePrompt: extract all email addresses from the body using \b(\w+)@(\w+\.\w+)\b with the g flag
